title: spotify.player.current value
description: Wire contract for the now-playing stream: the exact JSON the producer (ingestion/spotify/producer_current.py) emits and the ClickHouse Kafka-engine table (warehouse/ddl/10_spotify_current_track.sql, bronze.kafka_spotify_player_current) ingests. Registered in the Redpanda Schema Registry under subject 'spotify.player.current-value'; enforced producer-side before produce (rejects go to spotify.player.current.dlq). Closed and fully required on purpose: a renamed, added, or retyped field is a contract violation, not a silent drop. Integer bounds are Int32 because that is what the ClickHouse columns hold. CI parity check: scripts/check_stream_contract.py.

captured_at.description: UTC, ClickHouse 'basic' DateTime64(3) form (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.mmm). ISO-8601 'T'/'Z' forms are rejected by the Kafka-engine parser and once silently emptied the table; this pattern makes that a loud DLQ event instead.

track_id.description: Empty string for local files (Spotify returns null ids for them).
